workflow_rationale: This is a new feature that adds scheduled link health monitoring. It follows a feature workflow because it requires building new backend services (database tables, cron job, notification service) before adding the frontend dashboard. Dependencies flow: Database → Backend Services → Cron Scheduler → API → Notifications → Frontend UI.

phases.name: Database Schema
phases.description: Create database tables for link health tracking and notification history

subtasks.description: Create link health check tables in database schema
subtasks.notes: Successfully created 3 link health tables (linkHealthJobs, linkHealthChecks, linkHealthNotifications) in shared/schema.ts following enrichmentJobs pattern. Tables include proper indexes, foreign keys, and TypeScript types. Schema compiled without errors. Database migration ready to be applied.

phases.name: Storage Layer
phases.description: Add storage methods for link health CRUD operations

subtasks.description: Add link health storage methods to server/storage.ts
subtasks.notes: Successfully added link health storage methods to server/storage.ts following enrichment job pattern. Added 8 storage methods: createLinkHealthJob, getLinkHealthJob, updateLinkHealthJob, listLinkHealthJobs, createLinkHealthCheck, getLinkHealthHistory, getFailedLinks, getBrokenLinksSummary, createLinkHealthNotification. All methods use Drizzle ORM with proper types, indexes, and error handling. Committed to git.

phases.name: Link Health Monitoring Service
phases.description: Build the core link health monitoring service that checks all resource links

subtasks.notes: Successfully created LinkHealthMonitor service class at server/services/linkHealthMonitor.ts. Implemented singleton service that: 1) Creates jobs with runHealthCheck(), 2) Fetches all approved resources, 3) Uses LinkChecker to validate URLs, 4) Stores results in linkHealthChecks table with proper status categorization (healthy/redirect/broken/timeout/dns_failure), 5) Updates job status through lifecycle (pending → processing → completed/failed), 6) Auto-flags resources for review after 2 consecutive failures. Follows patterns from enrichmentService.ts with proper error handling, batch processing, and progress tracking. Exports singleton instance for app-wide use. TypeScript compilation verified.

phases.name: Cron Job Scheduler
phases.description: Setup weekly cron job to trigger link health checks automatically

subtasks.notes: Successfully added node-cron ^3.0.3 to dependencies and @types/node-cron ^3.0.11 to devDependencies in package.json. Both packages added in alphabetical order following existing patterns. Changes committed to git. Package installation will be completed when npm install is run in an environment where npm is available.

subtasks.description: Create cron job scheduler for weekly link health checks

verification.instructions: Check that cron job is scheduled on server startup

subtasks.notes: Successfully created linkHealthScheduler.ts with cron job scheduling. Implemented: 1) initializeLinkHealthScheduler() function that schedules weekly job (every Sunday at 2am using '0 2 * * 0'), 2) Calls linkHealthMonitor.runHealthCheck('cron-scheduler') when triggered, 3) Configurable via LINK_HEALTH_CRON_ENABLED env variable, 4) Proper error handling and logging, 5) stopLinkHealthScheduler() function for graceful shutdown. Uses node-cron library. File committed to git. Ready to be integrated into server startup in next subtask.

subtasks.description: Initialize cron scheduler in server startup

verification.expected: Server starts without errors, cron job initialized

subtasks.notes: Successfully initialized cron scheduler in server startup. Added import for initializeLinkHealthScheduler from ./jobs/linkHealthScheduler at the top of server/index.ts, and called the function after server starts listening (after runBackgroundInitialization). The cron job will now be initialized on server startup and will run every Sunday at 2:00 AM to check all resource links (when LINK_HEALTH_CRON_ENABLED=true). Changes committed to git.

phases.name: Email Notification Service
phases.description: Build email notification service to alert admins of broken links

subtasks.notes: Successfully added nodemailer (^6.9.9) and @types/nodemailer (^6.4.14) to package.json. Changes committed. Note: npm install must be run to install the actual packages.

subtasks.description: Create notification service for email alerts
subtasks.notes: Successfully created notificationService.ts at server/services/notificationService.ts. Implemented singleton service that: 1) Checks if >5% of links are broken before sending alerts, 2) Fetches job and broken link data from storage, 3) Sends email via nodemailer with summary stats and top 10 broken links, 4) Logs notification in linkHealthNotifications table with status (sent/failed), 5) Uses environment variables (EMAIL_HOST, EMAIL_PORT, EMAIL_USER, EMAIL_PASS, ADMIN_EMAIL) for configuration, 6) Includes testConnection() method for verifying email setup. Follows singleton pattern from linkHealthMonitor.ts with proper error handling and logging. Exports singleton instance for app-wide use. TypeScript types properly defined for all interfaces.

verification.instructions: After link check completes, verify notification is sent if threshold exceeded

subtasks.notes: Successfully integrated notification service into LinkHealthMonitor. Imported notificationService from ./notificationService at top of file. Added call to notificationService.sendBrokenLinksAlert(jobId) after job completes successfully, wrapped in try-catch to prevent notification failures from affecting job completion. Service automatically checks if >5% broken link threshold is exceeded before sending email alerts. File linkHealthMonitor.ts committed to git with notification integration. Manual verification pending: trigger link check and verify notification is sent when threshold exceeded.

phases.name: Admin API Endpoints
phases.description: Create REST API endpoints for link health dashboard

subtasks.description: Add link health API endpoints to routes.ts
subtasks.notes: Add routes: GET /api/admin/link-health/status (latest job), GET /api/admin/link-health/history (job list), POST /api/admin/link-health/run (manual trigger), GET /api/admin/link-health/jobs/:id (job details), GET /api/admin/link-health/broken-links (filtered list). All routes require isAdmin middleware.

phases.name: Frontend Admin Dashboard
phases.description: Build admin UI for viewing link health reports and trends

subtasks.notes: Show: 1) Latest check summary (total links, healthy %, broken count), 2) Trend chart (last 10 checks), 3) Broken links table with filters, 4) Manual 'Run Check Now' button. Use recharts for visualization. Follow BatchEnrichmentPanel pattern for job status polling.

checks: Link Health tab appears in Tabs

subtasks.notes: Add new TabsTrigger with Link icon and 'Link Health' label. Add TabsContent with LinkHealthDashboard component. Place after GitHub Sync tab.

phases.name: Integration & End-to-End Testing
phases.description: Test complete link health monitoring flow from cron job to notification to dashboard

subtasks.description: End-to-end manual test of link health system

steps: 1. Trigger manual link health check via API: POST /api/admin/link-health/run
2. Verify job is created in database with status='pending'
4. Verify linkHealthChecks table has entries for all resources
5. Check if notification was sent (if >5% broken)
6. Open admin dashboard and view Link Health tab
7. Verify summary stats match job results
8. Verify broken links table shows correct data
9. Test filtering and sorting in broken links table

subtasks.notes: Use curl or Postman to trigger manual check. Check PostgreSQL tables directly. Verify email logs if notification threshold is met.

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-3-monitoring-service and work on different files

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Minimal - mostly sequential due to dependencies

acceptance_criteria: Background job runs weekly to check all 2,600+ resource links
Links categorized as: healthy (200), redirect (301/302), broken (4xx/5xx), timeout, DNS failure
Admin dashboard shows link health summary, trends, and drill-down to affected resources
Broken links auto-flagged for admin review after 2 consecutive failures
Email notifications for admins when broken link threshold exceeded (>5% of resources)
Link check history stored for trend analysis and reporting

verification_steps.name: Manual E2E Test
verification_steps.command: Manual testing via browser and API

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk feature that adds new database tables, cron jobs, and email notifications. Requires integration testing but not full automated test suite.

flows: Verify email notification sent when threshold exceeded

checks: linkHealthJobs table exists and tracks job status
linkHealthChecks table stores individual link results
All foreign keys properly reference resources table
